prompt: What should the dedicated test repository be named / who provisions it? The design assumes a persistent repo (placeholder `spinyfin/checkleft-e2e`) whose default branch holds only machine-managed scaffold. Confirm the org/name and who creates it + seeds branch protection (none).
Which credential should drive the test repo from mono CI (open/edit/close PRs, dispatch, read check-runs, download artifacts, upload assets)? Local runs use the developer's gh token either way.
choices: GitHub App installed on the test repo (short-lived, repo-scoped tokens; key in Buildkite secrets)
Fine-grained PAT stored in Buildkite secrets (simpler to set up, human must rotate)

prompt: For v1, should the harness build the checkleft-under-test itself (from a sha or path, reusing the `checkleft_musl` target), rather than blocking on mono CI publishing reusable per-PR artifacts (T1310/T1313/T1314)? 'Yes' = build-in-harness now, consume per-PR artifacts later as F1.
